full_response:
The product of two matrices \([N]\) and \([M]\) is defined using summation notation for each entry. For \(3 \times 3\) matrices, the \((i,j)\)-th element of the product \([N] \cdot [M]\) is given by summing over the index \(k\) from 1 to 3.

\[
\boxed{([N]\cdot[M])_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{kj}}
\]

Confidence: 100%
